recipes.name: Sweet potato toast & soft-boiled egg
recipes.description: Moderate glycemic index carb base + light protein. Ideal 60 min before training.
steps: Cut the sweet potato into 1 cm slices and pan-fry 5 min per side in olive oil.
Boil the eggs for 6 min for a soft-boiled center, then transfer to cold water to stop cooking.
Place the peeled and halved eggs on top of the sweet potato toasts.
Season with salt, pepper, and smoked paprika. Serve immediately.

recipes.name: Banana & honey porridge
recipes.description: A timeless classic. Steady energy release and easy to digest.
steps: Pour the oats and milk into a saucepan over medium heat.
Stir constantly for 4–5 min until creamy.
Slice the banana and arrange on top of the porridge.
Drizzle with honey and sprinkle with cinnamon.
recipes.tip: To lighten: 1 tsp honey instead of 1 tbsp and unsweetened plant milk (~-100 kcal).

recipes.name: Whole wheat bread, almond butter & banana
recipes.description: The perfect combo: complex carbs + healthy fats + fast sugars at the end.
steps: Lightly toast the bread slices.
Spread generously with almond butter.
Top with banana slices. Serve.

recipes.name: Basmati rice & canned tuna
recipes.description: A savory, high-density option for those who can't stomach sweet food before training.
steps: Cook the rice according to the package (or use pre-cooked rice).
Drain the tuna and flake it into a bowl with soy sauce and lemon juice.
Toss with the warm rice.

recipes.name: Oat, milk & banana smoothie
recipes.description: Light and easy to digest. Great for those with a sensitive stomach before training.
steps: Blend all ingredients together until smooth.
Drink immediately. Add a splash of milk if too thick.

recipes.name: Brown rice, grilled chicken & broccoli bowl
recipes.description: The post-training classic. Optimal protein-to-carb ratio for muscle recovery.
steps: Cook the brown rice according to the package (20 min, or use pre-cooked).
Slice the chicken, season with salt and garlic, and pan-fry for 6–7 min in olive oil.
Steam the broccoli for 5 min (it should remain slightly crisp).
Assemble in a bowl and drizzle with a little olive oil.

recipes.name: 3-egg mushroom omelette
recipes.description: Quick, protein-packed, low in carbs. Ideal when you prefer to skip starchy foods after training.
steps: Sauté the sliced mushrooms in olive oil for 4–5 min.
Beat the eggs, season, and pour over the mushrooms.
Sprinkle with cheese and fold the omelette halfway through cooking.
Cook for another 1–2 min depending on desired texture.

recipes.name: Grilled salmon, quinoa & broccoli
recipes.description: Anti-inflammatory omega-3s + complete proteins. Excellent for deep muscle recovery.

ingredients.qty: Salt, dill or herbes de Provence

steps: Rinse and cook the quinoa in 160ml of salted water, covered, for 15 min.
Season the salmon and pan-fry for 4 min per side.
Steam the broccoli for 5 min.
Plate the quinoa, broccoli and salmon. Drizzle with lemon juice and olive oil.
recipes.tip: To lighten: ½ tbsp oil and 100g salmon instead of 150g (~-200 kcal).

recipes.name: Whey & banana recovery smoothie
recipes.description: The express post-workout option. Drink within 30 min after training.
steps: Blend all ingredients together.
Drink immediately after your workout.

recipes.name: Chicken & avocado wrap
recipes.description: Easy to prep ahead. Protein + healthy fats + carbs — all in one hand.
steps: Spread the fromage blanc over the tortilla.
Layer the salad, chicken, and sliced avocado.
Season, roll tightly, and cut in half.

recipes.name: Fromage blanc, mixed berries & granola
recipes.description: A light post-workout option for short sessions or when appetite is low.

ingredients.item: Fromage blanc 0% or 3.2% (or plain low-fat yogurt)

steps: Pour the fromage blanc into a bowl.
Top with mixed berries.
Sprinkle granola just before eating (to keep it crunchy).

recipes.name: Oat & banana pancakes (3 ingredients)
recipes.description: No flour, no added sugar. Fluffy texture and naturally sweet.
steps: Mash the banana with a fork, then mix with the beaten eggs and oats.
Let rest for 2 min to allow the oats to absorb.
Cook small pancakes over medium heat, 2–3 min per side.
Serve with fresh fruit or honey.

recipes.name: Veggie egg muffins (meal prep)
recipes.description: Prep on Sunday, eat all week. High protein and free from unnecessary carbs.
steps: Preheat the oven to 180°C (350°F). Grease a muffin tin.
Divide the vegetables among the muffin cups.
Beat the eggs, season, and pour over the vegetables. Sprinkle with cheese.
Bake for 20 min. Keeps for 4 days in the fridge.

recipes.name: Greek yogurt, homemade granola & fruit
recipes.description: Probiotics + protein + complex carbs. The perfect base for a strong start.
steps: Pour the yogurt into a bowl.
Add the fruit, then the granola.
Sprinkle with chia seeds if using. Eat immediately.
recipes.tip: To lighten: 0% Greek yogurt instead of full-fat (~-130 kcal).

recipes.name: Avocado toast & poached egg
recipes.description: Trendy and effective. Healthy fats + protein + complex carbs to keep you fueled all morning.

ingredients.item: Whole wheat bread or rye bread

ingredients.qty: Flaky sea salt, pepper, chili flakes (optional)

steps: Bring water to a gentle simmer with a splash of white vinegar.
Create a swirl and gently slide in the egg. Cook for 3 min for a runny yolk.
Mash the avocado on the toasted bread, add a squeeze of lemon, season.
Top with the poached egg and a pinch of flaky salt.
recipes.tip: To lighten: ½ avocado is enough (~-160 kcal).

recipes.name: Protein açaí bowl
recipes.description: Concentrated antioxidants + protein. Stunning to look at and even better to eat.
steps: Blend the açaí, banana, and plant-based milk until thick and smooth.
Pour into a bowl.
Decorate with your chosen toppings.

recipes.name: Red lentil dahl
recipes.description: Anti-inflammatory, rich in plant protein and iron. Perfect for rest days.
steps: Sauté the garlic and ginger in oil for 2 min. Add the spices and stir for 30 sec.
Add the rinsed lentils, tomatoes, and coconut milk.
Simmer over medium heat for 20–25 min, stirring regularly, until the lentils are soft.
Adjust seasoning. Serve with rice or naan bread.
recipes.tip: To lighten: light coconut milk (~80 kcal/100mL) instead of full-fat (~-110 kcal/serving).

recipes.name: Miso soup with tofu & edamame
recipes.description: Light, remineralizing, and probiotic-rich. Perfect for the evening after an intense session.
steps: Dissolve the miso paste in the hot water (do not boil — this preserves the probiotics).
Add the diced tofu, edamame, and soy sauce.
Serve topped with sliced green onion.

recipes.name: Chickpea, avocado & feta salad
recipes.description: Plant protein + healthy fats + calcium. No cooking needed, ready in 5 min.
steps: Rinse and drain the chickpeas.
Toss all ingredients together in a bowl.
Season with lemon juice, olive oil, salt, pepper, and cumin.

recipes.name: Vegetable & chickpea curry
recipes.description: Turmeric and ginger make this an actively anti-inflammatory meal. Comforting and satisfying.
steps: Sauté the vegetables in a pan for 5 min.
Add the spices and stir for 30 sec.
Add the chickpeas and coconut milk. Simmer for 15 min.
Serve over cooked basmati rice.
recipes.tip: To lighten: 200mL coconut milk instead of 400mL, or use the light version (~-180 kcal).

recipes.name: Quinoa & mint tabbouleh
recipes.description: Complete proteins from quinoa + fresh herbs. Light, digestible, perfect for the evening.
steps: Cook the quinoa in 320ml of salted water for 12 min. Let cool.
Finely chop the cucumber, tomatoes, and herbs.
Toss everything together and season with lemon juice, olive oil, and salt.
Can be prepared the night before (better the next day).
recipes.tip: To lighten: 1 tbsp oil instead of 2 (~-60 kcal).

recipes.name: Homemade hummus & crudités
recipes.description: Homemade hummus in 5 min in the blender. Way tastier than store-bought.
steps: Blend all hummus ingredients with 3–4 tbsp of cold water.
Blend for a long time (3 min) for an ultra-creamy texture.
Serve in a bowl, drizzled with olive oil and paprika.
Accompany with crudités cut into sticks.

recipes.name: Oat & dark chocolate energy balls
recipes.description: The ideal snack to prep on Sunday for the whole week. Zero cooking required.
steps: Mix all the ingredients together in a bowl.
Refrigerate for 20 min to firm up.
Roll into walnut-sized balls.
Store in the fridge for up to 1 week (or freeze).

recipes.name: Hard-boiled eggs & fromage blanc
recipes.description: The simplest, most effective protein snack. No excuses not to have it.
steps: Boil the eggs for 10 min. Cool under cold water, then peel.
Season the fromage blanc.
Eat together. Can be prepped in advance and kept for 3 days.

recipes.name: Cottage cheese, nuts & honey
recipes.description: Slow proteins (casein) + healthy fats. Ideal in the evening for overnight recovery.
steps: Pour the cottage cheese into a bowl.
Add the nuts and honey.
Sprinkle with cinnamon if desired.

recipes.name: Homemade beef & cheddar burger
recipes.description: A real homemade burger. No reason to hold back — the difference from fast food is all about ingredient quality and portion size.
steps: Shape the ground beef into a ball, season, and press gently into a patty. Don't overwork the meat.
Cook over high heat for 2–3 min per side for a medium patty. Place the cheddar on top 30 sec before the end, cover to melt.
Lightly toast the bun, cut in half.
Assemble: sauce on both bun halves, lettuce, tomato, patty. Serve immediately.
recipes.tip: To lighten: 2 tbsp mayo instead of 4 and 5% lean beef (~-300 kcal).

recipes.name: Pasta carbonara (the real recipe)
recipes.description: The real deal: no cream. Guanciale or lardons, egg yolks, pecorino. Simple, technical, perfect.
steps: Cook the pasta al dente in heavily salted water. Reserve 2 ladlefuls of pasta water.
Cook the guanciale in a large pan over medium heat until lightly crisp. Remove from heat.
Whisk together the egg yolks, whole egg, pecorino, and plenty of black pepper in a bowl.

Drain the pasta and add to the pan with the guanciale, off the heat. Pour the egg-cheese mixture over and toss vigorously, adding pasta water a spoonful at a time to create a creamy sauce. Never put back on the heat.
recipes.tip: To lighten: 60g smoked bacon instead of guanciale and 40g pecorino (~-130 kcal).

recipes.name: Pasta with basil pesto & parmesan
recipes.description: Ten minutes, zero technique. Homemade pesto makes all the difference.
steps: Cook the pasta al dente in heavily salted water.
Blend the basil, pine nuts, garlic, and oil into a paste. Add the parmesan and pulse briefly. Season.
Reserve 1 ladleful of pasta water. Drain the pasta.
Toss with the pesto off the heat. Loosen with a little pasta water if needed.
recipes.tip: To lighten: 3 tbsp oil and a splash of pasta water to loosen the pesto (~-300 kcal).

recipes.name: Roast chicken with lemon & garlic
recipes.description: The ultimate Sunday roast. One homemade roast chicken gives you 6 portions for the week.
steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C (400°F). Take the chicken out of the fridge 30 min before.
Rub the outside with olive oil and generous salt and pepper. Stuff the herbs and halved lemon inside.
Arrange the garlic cloves around the chicken in the roasting pan.
Roast for 1h20, basting every 20 min. Check doneness by piercing a thigh — the juices should run clear.

recipes.name: Steak & sweet potato fries
recipes.description: The homemade version is incomparable. Oven-baked sweet potato fries are a genuinely great swap — no cheating involved.
steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C (400°F). Cut the sweet potato into sticks, toss with oil, paprika, and salt. Roast for 30 min, flipping halfway.
Take the steak out of the fridge 15 min before. Season just before cooking.
Sear over very high heat for 2 min per side for rare, or 3 min for medium. Rest for 3 min before slicing.
Serve immediately with the fries.
recipes.tip: To lighten: 1 tbsp oil for the fries and cook the steak fat-free (~-160 kcal).

recipes.name: Quick homemade ramen
recipes.description: Not a 4-hour restaurant ramen — a 25-min version that's still delicious and hearty.

ingredients.item: Chicken breast or thin pork belly

steps: Boil the eggs for 6 min, cool immediately in cold water. Peel and halve.
Bring the broth to a simmer, add soy sauce and miso. Taste and adjust.
Cook the noodles separately according to the package. Brown the meat in a pan.
Divide the noodles between bowls, ladle the hot broth over, then top with meat, egg, and toppings. Finish with a drizzle of sesame oil.

recipes.name: Chicken & cheese quesadillas
recipes.description: Ready in 15 minutes. Crispy, gooey, filling. Perfect for nights when inspiration runs low.

ingredients.item: Cooked shredded chicken (leftover roast chicken works great)

steps: Toss the chicken with the bell pepper and spices.
Heat a large dry pan over medium heat.
Place one tortilla in the pan, add the chicken and cheese on one half, fold over. Cook for 2–3 min per side until golden.
Cut into triangles and serve with sour cream or guacamole.
recipes.tip: To lighten: 1 folded tortilla and 30g cheese instead of 60g (~-200 kcal).

recipes.name: Parmesan & mushroom risotto
recipes.description: The ultimate comfort dish. Needs 30 min of attention but no difficult technique.
steps: Sauté the shallot and mushrooms in olive oil for 5 min.
Add the rice and stir for 2 min to toast it.
Pour in the white wine and let it absorb.
Add the hot broth one ladle at a time, stirring constantly. Each ladle must be absorbed before adding the next. This takes about 18–20 min.
Off the heat, stir in butter and parmesan vigorously (the mantecatura). Serve immediately.
recipes.tip: To lighten: half the butter and parmesan at the end (~-90 kcal).

recipes.name: Spicy beef tacos & guacamole
recipes.description: Colorful, convivial, eaten with your hands. The ideal format when you don't want to weigh every gram.
steps: Brown the ground beef with the spices until cooked through. Season.
Mash the avocado with lime juice, salt, and a pinch of cumin — quick guacamole.
Warm the tortillas directly over the flame or in a dry pan.
Assemble: guacamole, spicy beef, red onion, cilantro, sour cream.

recipes.name: Homemade tomato & mozzarella pizza
recipes.description: Made with the homemade pizza dough (see Bases). A real pizza, not a lighter version.
steps: Preheat the oven to maximum heat (250°C / 480°F or higher) with the baking sheet inside.
Stretch the dough thinly over floured parchment paper, leaving the edge slightly thicker.
Spread the tomato sauce, tear the mozzarella on top. Season.
Bake for 10–12 min until the crust is golden and the mozzarella is bubbling. Add fresh basil and olive oil right out of the oven.
recipes.tip: To lighten: 100g mozzarella instead of 150g and 1 tbsp oil (~-180 kcal).

recipes.name: Classic crêpes
recipes.description: The base recipe. Thin, golden, to fill as you like — sweet or savory.
steps: Combine flour, salt, and sugar. Make a well and add the beaten eggs in the center.
Gradually whisk in the milk to avoid lumps. Stir in the melted butter.
Rest for 30 min if possible (not mandatory).
Cook in a lightly buttered pan over medium heat, 1 min per side.

recipes.name: Cinnamon French toast & maple syrup
recipes.description: Day-old brioche transformed into something exceptional. Ready in 10 minutes.
steps: Beat the eggs with the milk, cinnamon, and vanilla.
Soak the brioche slices for 30 seconds per side.
Cook over medium heat in foaming butter for 2–3 min per side until golden.
Serve with maple syrup, fresh fruit, or powdered sugar.
recipes.tip: To lighten: whole-grain bread and a drizzle of maple syrup instead of a portion (~-100 kcal).

recipes.name: Chocolate mousse
recipes.description: 3 ingredients, zero compromise. The purest mousse imaginable — dark chocolate, whipped egg whites, sugar.
steps: Melt the chocolate in a bain-marie or microwave in 30-second bursts. Let cool slightly.
Whip the egg whites to stiff peaks. When they form soft peaks, gradually add the sugar and continue beating.
Gently fold the egg whites into the melted chocolate in three additions, lifting with a spatula. Do not beat — you want to preserve the air.
Pour into ramekins or glasses. Refrigerate for at least 2 hours before serving.
recipes.author: Erwan

recipes.name: Oat & dark chocolate cookies
recipes.description: Crispy on the outside, chewy in the middle. Real cookies, not sports bars.
steps: Preheat the oven to 170°C (340°F). Beat the butter and sugar until creamy.
Add the egg and mix. Fold in the flour, oats, salt, and baking soda.
Stir in the chocolate chips. Roll into walnut-sized balls and press lightly onto the baking sheet.
Bake for 12–14 min. Let cool on the tray (they firm up as they cool).

recipes.name: Brownie à la papy Brossard
recipes.description: The family recipe. Dense, fudgy, with flaky salt that makes all the difference. More chocolate than classic versions.
steps: Melt the chocolate with the butter. Add the oil. Let cool slightly.
Whisk the eggs with the sugar until pale and fluffy.
Fold the melted chocolate into the egg-sugar mixture.
Add the sifted flour and baking soda. Mix without overworking.
Stir in the flaky salt and chocolate chips.
Pour into a greased pan. Bake at 180°C (350°F): 20 min for a large dish, 25 min for a smaller pan. The center should still jiggle slightly.
recipes.author: Famille

recipes.name: Quick tiramisu
recipes.description: No lecithin, no light mascarpone. The authentic Italian recipe, in a simplified version.
steps: Whisk the egg yolks with the sugar until pale. Fold in the mascarpone with a spatula.
Beat the egg whites to stiff peaks. Gently fold into the mascarpone cream.
Quickly dip the ladyfingers in the coffee (1 second per side — don't soak them).
Layer the cookies and cream in a dish, finishing with cream. Refrigerate for at least 3 hours.
Dust with cocoa powder just before serving.
recipes.tip: To lighten: replace half the mascarpone with ricotta (~-110 kcal/serving).

recipes.name: Banana nice cream
recipes.description: 2 ingredients, ice cream texture, zero guilt. Frozen blended banana does all the work.
steps: Blend the frozen banana chunks alone for 3–4 min, scraping the sides regularly.
The texture will first look crumbly, then turn creamy like ice cream.
Add peanut butter or cocoa if desired, blend for another 30 sec.
Serve immediately for a soft-serve texture, or freeze for 30 min for a firmer scoop.
recipes.tip: To lighten: 2 bananas instead of 3 (~215 kcal/serving).

recipes.name: Homemade pasta dough
recipes.description: The foundation for all fresh pasta. Requires a pasta machine (or serious motivation with a rolling pin). The result is worlds apart from dried pasta.
recipes.note: Values for the dough alone, without sauce. Add 200–300 kcal depending on the topping.

ingredients.item: Tipo 00 flour (or all-purpose as a substitute)

steps: Make a well with the flour. Add the yolks and whole eggs in the center.
Gradually incorporate the flour from the edges using a fork.
Knead by hand for 8–10 min until you have a smooth, elastic, non-sticky dough. Add a few drops of water if too dry.
Wrap in plastic wrap. Refrigerate for at least 1 hour (up to 24h — longer means better texture).
Divide into 4 portions. Roll through a pasta machine (setting 1 → 5 or 6) or very thinly by hand.
Cut into tagliatelle, fettuccine, or use for lasagna, ravioli, etc.
recipes.author: Erwan

recipes.name: Homemade pizza dough
recipes.description: A reliable home recipe — easier to handle than a high-hydration Neapolitan dough, with great structure thanks to olive oil.
recipes.note: Values for 1 individual pizza base (half the recipe). Toppings are extra.
steps: Dissolve the yeast in the warm water. Let activate for 5 min.
In a large bowl, mix flour and salt. Add the water + yeast and olive oil.
Knead by hand for 10 min until you have a smooth, soft, slightly elastic dough.
Cover with a damp cloth. Let rise for 1 hour at room temperature (or overnight in the fridge for more flavor).
Divide into 2 dough balls. Stretch thinly by hand or with a rolling pin. Top and bake at 250°C (480°F) on a preheated baking sheet for 10–12 min.
recipes.author: Erwan

recipes.name: Marinated salmon poke bowl
recipes.description: The post-workout dish that doesn't look like athlete food. Vinegared rice, soy-honey-ginger marinated salmon. The homemade version is incomparable.

ingredients.item: Avocado, edamame, cucumber, radish, sesame seeds

steps: Cook the rice in a rice cooker (equal volume of water to rice). Meanwhile, gently heat the rice vinegar with the sugar and salt until dissolved — do not boil.
Once cooked, gently fold the vinegar mixture into the warm rice. Let cool to room temperature.
Cut the salmon into cubes. Marinate for 15–20 min in soy sauce + honey + ginger + lemon juice.
Assemble the bowls: rice, marinated salmon, toppings. Drizzle with the remaining marinade.
recipes.author: Erwan
recipes.tip: 60g of raw rice per serving gives a lighter portion (~-90 kcal).

recipes.name: Homemade flan pâtissier
recipes.description: The bakery flan, made at home. Creamy, vanilla-scented, with a lightly crisp pastry base.

ingredients.item: Shortcrust or puff pastry (homemade or store-bought)

steps: Mix the 3 eggs with 125ml of cold milk and the cornstarch until smooth with no lumps.
In a saucepan, heat the remaining 875ml of milk with the sugar, vanilla sugar, and split vanilla bean. Bring to a boil.
Pour the hot milk over the egg mixture, whisking constantly. Return to the saucepan over medium heat, stirring continuously until thickened.
Line a 24–26 cm pan with the pastry. Pour the custard filling on top.
Bake at 180°C (350°F) for 40 min. The top should be lightly golden and still jiggle slightly in the center.
Let cool completely before unmolding.
recipes.author: Erwan

recipes.name: Buckwheat galette dough
recipes.description: The Breton buckwheat galette batter. Simple, no butter in the dough, leaving all the room for the fillings. The version that works at home.
recipes.note: Calories for one galette alone, without filling. Add 150–400 kcal depending on the filling (complete, chicken-cheese, etc.)
steps: Mix the flours, salt, and pepper in a large bowl. Make a well.
Add the egg and oil in the center. Incorporate by stirring from the center outward.
Gradually add the water to avoid lumps. The batter should be fluid.
Rest for at least 1 hour (or overnight in the fridge — the galettes will be better).
Cook on a very hot, lightly oiled crêpe pan or non-stick skillet for 1–2 min per side.
recipes.author: Erwan

recipes.name: Chickpea & peanut butter cookies
recipes.description: The recipe that surprises everyone. No chickpea taste, cookie texture, protein snack macros. Zero flour.

ingredients.item: Caramel whey protein (or cocoa powder as a substitute)

steps: Blend the undrained chickpeas with the peanut butter, honey, and whey until smooth.
Fold in a few chocolate chips with a spatula.
Shape into cookies on a sheet of parchment paper. Press more chips on top.
Bake for 10–12 min at 180°C (350°F). Let cool completely before handling.

recipes.name: Homemade crêpe batter
recipes.description: The generous version — more butter, more eggs. Rich, golden crêpes. Two versions in the notes; this is the fuller one.
recipes.note: For a lighter version: 250g flour, 3 eggs, 60g sugar, 5g salt, 400ml milk. Thinner result, no butter.
steps: Melt the butter and let it cool slightly.
Beat the eggs in a large bowl. Add the flour and sugar, mix.
Stir in the oil and melted butter.
Gradually add 50cl of milk, whisking to avoid lumps.
Rest for 1 hour (mandatory for this recipe).
Add the remaining 25cl of milk if the batter is too thick. Cook over medium heat in a lightly buttered crêpe pan.
recipes.author: Erwan
recipes.tip: Note: the original recipe combines oil + butter, which is generous. Keep only the butter to lighten.
